app.name: Droidsmith
app.description: Inspect devices, stage actions, and keep every risky Android operation traceable.

runtime.notAttachedHint: Device IPC is available when the app is launched with npm run tauri:dev.
runtime.notAttachedPrefix: Device IPC is available when the app is launched with

devices.title: Devices
devices.description: Scan USB and TCP/IP targets, confirm ADB readiness, and see enough context to choose the right device before any action runs.
devices.selectHint: Select a device to view its dashboard. Review state and transport before launching any action.
devices.authorizeOneBody: Device {{serial}} is connected but has not been authorized yet.
devices.authorizeManyBody: The following devices are connected but have not been authorized yet:
devices.step1: Look at the device screen for the Allow USB debugging? dialog.
devices.step2: Optionally check Always allow from this computer to skip this next time.
devices.step3: Tap Allow, then click Refresh below.
devices.launchDesktopTitle: Launch the desktop shell to scan hardware
devices.launchDesktopBodyPrefix: This browser preview can render the interface, but device IPC only exists inside Tauri. Start the desktop shell with
devices.launchDesktopBodySuffix: when you want Droidsmith to talk to ADB.
devices.noAdbBodyMiddle: Android Studio defaults, Homebrew, and common Linux package-manager locations. Install Android platform tools or run
devices.noDevicesStep1: Connect USB and enable developer options.
devices.noDevicesStep3: Or use the Wireless tab for Wi-Fi pairing.
devices.linuxPermsBody: One or more devices report no permissions. On Linux, add a udev rule for your device's USB vendor ID:
devices.linuxPermsExamples: with your device's vendor ID, for example
devices.mustAuthorize: Device must be authorized before it can be selected
devices.reconnectUsb: Reconnect the USB cable and wait a few seconds.
devices.fileTransferMode: Ensure USB mode is set to File Transfer / MTP; some devices block debugging in charge-only mode.

controls.screenshotBody: Capture the current screen to a local PNG file.
controls.processManagerBody: Snapshot of running processes sorted by memory usage.
controls.noProcessesBody: Load a process snapshot to inspect memory-heavy apps.
controls.noMatchingProcessesBody: Clear the filter or search for another process name.
controls.fileManagerBody: Browse, pull, and inspect files on the device.
controls.noDirectoryBodySuffix: to inspect device files and pull local copies.
controls.emptyDirectoryBody: This path has no visible entries.
controls.connectionsReadFailed: Couldn't read connections: {{message}}. Some devices restrict ss/netstat without root.
controls.noConnectionsBody: Load active sockets to review endpoints and owning processes.
controls.noMatchingConnectionsBody: Clear the filter or search for another address, process, or state.

wireless.title: Wireless
wireless.description: Pair Android 11+ devices over Wi-Fi, discover mDNS pairing/connect endpoints, and attach paired devices without leaving Droidsmith.
wireless.name: Name
wireless.desktopRequiredBody: Wireless ADB commands run inside the Tauri runtime.
wireless.noAdbBody: Install Android platform tools or stage the bundled sidecar.
wireless.noServicesBody: mDNS did not return pairing or connect targets on this network.
wireless.discoveredServicesBody: Pairing services use the QR code above; connect services attach already-paired devices.

apps.title: Apps
apps.description: Review installed packages with precise filters, and a preview-before-apply workflow for disable, enable, uninstall, and data-clear actions.
apps.installedPackagesBody: Actions preview before applying. Disable and enable are reversible via the journal.
apps.backupLimitations: Android's legacy adb backup is app-controlled on modern devices: the device may require confirmation, some apps opt out, and a small or empty file can mean no usable data was exported.
apps.backupEmptyBody: ADB finished but the backup artifact is empty or missing. Treat this package as not backed up.
apps.backupHeaderOnlyBody: The .ab file holds only its header. Apps that target Android 12 (API 31) or newer opt out of the deprecated `adb backup`, so no private data was captured — only the APK. Treat this as an APK-only backup.
apps.desktopRequiredBody: Package management runs inside the Tauri runtime.
apps.noAuthorizedBody: Connect a device and accept the USB debugging prompt, then refresh.
apps.noMatchingPackagesBody: Adjust the search text or switch package filters to bring the list back.
apps.noPermissionsFoundBody: This package did not report runtime or install permissions.
apps.permissionToggleFailed: Couldn't change this permission: {{message}}. Some permissions are fixed by the system or app policy and can't be toggled here.
apps.journalBody: Recent package actions for this device. Disable and enable entries can be undone once.
apps.journalEmptyBody: Apply a package action and Droidsmith will record it here.

debloat.title: Debloat
debloat.description: Choose an OEM-aware pack, understand the risk before applying it, and keep every change reversible through the journal.
debloat.choosePackBody: Each pack targets a specific OEM or ROM. Packages are grouped by removal safety tier.
debloat.desktopRequiredBody: Debloat operations run inside the Tauri runtime.
debloat.noAuthorizedBody: Connect a device and accept the USB debugging prompt.
debloat.packErrorsBody: These bundled pack files were skipped. Fix or remove them; healthy packs below still work.
debloat.journalUndo: All changes are recorded in the per-device journal and can be undone from the Apps tab.
debloat.cancelledBeforeApply: Cancelled before this package was applied.
debloat.cancelledSummary: Cancellation stopped the queue after the current package finished.

verify.missing: Package disappeared after apply; expected it to remain installed and disabled.
verify.still_enabled: Package is still enabled after apply.

mirror.title: Mirror
mirror.description: Launch scrcpy sessions to mirror and control Android devices. Requires scrcpy installed on your system.
mirror.desktopRequiredBody: Mirror sessions run inside the Tauri runtime.
mirror.scrcpyInstallPrefix: Droidsmith uses scrcpy for device mirroring. Install it from
mirror.noAuthorizedBody: Connect and authorize a device to start mirroring.
mirror.sessionRunningBody: scrcpy is running as PID {{pid}}. Close the scrcpy window or stop it here to end the session.

console.title: Console
console.description: Run ADB shell commands with output history and command recall.
console.hint: Type a command below. Arrow keys recall history.
console.desktopRequiredBody: Shell commands run inside the Tauri runtime.
console.noAuthorizedBody: Connect and authorize a device to use the console.

logcat.title: Logcat
logcat.description: Tail device logs with level and text filters. Polling-based capture refreshes every 2 seconds.
logcat.desktopRequiredBody: Logcat runs inside the Tauri runtime.
logcat.noAuthorizedBody: Connect and authorize a device to view logs.
logcat.startHint: Press Start tail to begin capturing logs.

fastboot.title: Fastboot
fastboot.description: Inspect bootloader-mode devices, partition state, slots, and safety-critical variables before any destructive operation.
fastboot.safetyWarning: Fastboot operations can be destructive. Droidsmith intentionally does not expose flash, erase, or lock/unlock commands through the GUI. Use the Console tab with
fastboot.installSuffix: binary. Install it from the Android SDK or run the platform-tools fetch script.
fastboot.noBootloaderDevices: No devices in bootloader/fastboot mode. Reboot a device into the bootloader with
fastboot.queryHint: Press Query vars to read device information.
fastboot.queryAllFailed: Couldn't read any fastboot variables. The device may have left fastboot mode, or the bootloader rejected the query. Reconnect and try again.
fastboot.queryNoValues: The query completed but the device reported no values for these variables.

developerOptions.title: Enable Developer Options

content.0: Open Settings on your Android device.
content.1: Scroll to About phone or About tablet.
content.2: Tap Build number 7 times. You will see a toast saying you are now a developer.
content.3: Go back to Settings; Developer options is now visible.

developerOptions.tip: On Samsung: Settings > About phone > Software information > Build number.

usbDebugging.title: Enable USB Debugging

content.1: Scroll down to USB debugging and toggle it on.

usbDebugging.tip: On Xiaomi/MIUI, also enable USB debugging security settings to allow app installs.

drivers.title: Install USB Drivers (Windows)

content.0: Most devices work with the Google USB Driver from the Android SDK Manager.
content.1: Samsung devices use the Samsung USB Driver from samsung.com.
content.2: Other OEMs list ADB or USB drivers on their support pages.
content.3: macOS and Linux usually do not need extra drivers.

drivers.tip: On Linux, you may need a udev rule; Droidsmith shows the fix automatically when this happens.

authorize.title: Connect and Authorize

content.0: Connect your device via USB cable.
content.1: Your device will show an Allow USB debugging? dialog.
content.2: Check Always allow from this computer if this is your personal machine.
content.3: Tap Allow. The device should appear in Droidsmith's Devices tab.

authorize.tip: If the dialog does not appear, try a different USB cable; charge-only cables do not support data.

wireless.title: Wireless Debugging (Android 11+)

content.0: Open Settings > Developer options > Wireless debugging.
content.2: In Droidsmith, go to the Wireless tab.
content.3: Use QR pairing: scan the QR code shown in Droidsmith with your device.
content.4: Or use manual pairing: enter the host, port, and 6-digit code from your device.

wireless.tip: Both devices must be on the same Wi-Fi network. The pairing code changes each time.
